title: Default and Direct Exchanges, Work Queues, and Fair Dispatch · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs default and direct exchanges, work queues, and fair dispatch from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the direct-routing work distribution service. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for direct-routing work distribution service after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.
Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve.
Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Default Exchange Coupling
steps.instruction:
Order the Default Exchange Coupling RabbitMQ and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Default Exchange Coupling.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Default Exchange Coupling assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Default Exchange Coupling, keep Default Exchange Coupling connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A review messaging team handles case 2786 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Default Exchange Coupling has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Task Cost Fairness · changed case · Review Task Cost Fairness
steps.instruction:
review case — A review messaging team handles case 1358 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; retain a screen-reader-readable delivery and ownership trace; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Measure service time, prefetch, unacknowledged work, capacity, and tenant fairness instead of equating round robin with fair completion.
